rationale: The two papers are closely aligned in topic: the citing study measures TV advertising effects on online sessions/sales, while the cited paper is a channel-effectiveness case study that includes television and online channels with sales/profit outcomes. The citing note explicitly says it extends prior work on TV-to-online effects and attribution, so this cited JMR paper is a plausible foundational/comparison reference rather than an obviously incidental mention; with only DOI-level match and no quote context, it should be retained for full judgment.
